perturbation.original_text: REVENUE SHARE/FEE: Commencing on March 1, 2020 and thereafter, the Parties shall share revenue generated from Customer's consumer offerings, including but not limited to its content, technology, traffic, data, websites, apps, videos and podcasts, without offset and regardless of which Party is responsible for securing such revenue, as per the attached Schedule 1.
perturbation.changed_text: REVENUE SHARE/FEE: Commencing on March 1, 2020 and thereafter, the Parties shall share revenue generated from Customer's consumer offerings, including but not limited to its content, technology, traffic, data, websites, apps, videos and podcasts. Revenue sharing will be determined based on Schedule 1, but only if Kubient directly secures the revenue. Any revenue secured by the Customer independently will not be subject to revenue sharing.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that revenue sharing only applies if Kubient directly secures the revenue, which conflicts with the earlier statement that revenue sharing occurs 'without offset and regardless of which Party is responsible for securing such revenue.' This ambiguity creates uncertainty regarding which revenue streams are subject to sharing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REVENUE SHARE/FEE: Commencing on March 1, 2020 and thereafter, the Parties shall share revenue generated from Customer's consumer offerings, including but not limited to its content, technology, traffic, data, websites, apps, videos and podcasts. Revenue sharing will be determined based on Schedule 1, but only if Kubient directly secures the revenue. Any revenue secured by the Customer independently will not be subject to revenue sharing. RECONCILIATION/PAYMENT TERMS: The Parties agree to reconcile revenue numbers and the revenue share calculations no later than 10 days following the end of each calendar month during which the Services were rendered. Subject to the doctrine of sequential liability, payment shall be made to the appropriate Party net 30 days from the end of the calendar month.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text stated revenue sharing applied 'regardless of which Party is responsible for securing such revenue.' The modified text then states, 'Revenue sharing will be determined based on Schedule 1, but only if Kubient directly secures the revenue. Any revenue secured by the Customer independently will not be subject to revenue sharing.' This directly contradicts the original agreement and creates an ambiguity regarding which revenue streams are subject to sharing, which fits the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: NON-CIRCUMVENT: As part of the Services provided under the Agreement and any applicable exhibits, Kubient will be working with and introducing certain third-party advertisers, sponsors, agencies, media buyers, service providers, vendors, publishers, affiliates and/or media partners (collectively "Third-Party") with whom Customer does not have a pre-existing relationship. During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of one (1) year following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, Customer agrees not to work with, directly or indirectly, any Third-Party that Customer comes to know through disclosure by Kubient as part of the Services, without the express written consent of Kubient and compensation to Kubient under the applicable exhibit and/or schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: NON-CIRCUMVENT: As part of the Services provided under the Agreement and any applicable exhibits, Kubient will be working with and introducing certain third-party advertisers, sponsors, agencies, media buyers, service providers, vendors, publishers, affiliates and/or media partners (collectively "Third-Party") with whom Customer does not have a pre-existing relationship. During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of one (1) year following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, Customer agrees not to work with, directly or indirectly, any Third-Party that Customer comes to know through disclosure by Kubient as part of the Services, without the express written consent of Kubient. Compensation to Kubient for working with third-party is optional and depends on the success of the partnership.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Customer cannot work with any Third-Party introduced by Kubient without both consent and compensation to Kubient. The changed text introduces uncertainty by making compensation optional, which contradicts the original mandatory compensation requirement. This creates potential disputes on compensation for third-party partnerships.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-CIRCUMVENT: As part of the Services provided under the Agreement and any applicable exhibits, Kubient will be working with and introducing certain third-party advertisers, sponsors, agencies, media buyers, service providers, vendors, publishers, affiliates and/or media partners (collectively "Third-Party") with whom Customer does not have a pre-existing relationship. During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of one (1) year following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, Customer agrees not to work with, directly or indirectly, any Third-Party that Customer comes to know through disclosure by Kubient as part of the Services, without the express written consent of Kubient. Compensation to Kubient for working with third-party is optional and depends on the success of the partnership. Provided however, this provision shall not apply in the event Customer can establish, in writing, that it had a preexisting working relationship with such Third-Party prior to the Agreement and without Kubient's assistance. Customer acknowledges that this provision is reasonable and necessary for the protection of Kubient and that Kubient will be irrevocably damaged if such covenant is not specifically enforced.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires both consent and compensation for the Customer to work with a Third-Party introduced by Kubient. The changed text makes compensation optional, contradicting the original requirement. This directly creates uncertainty and potential disputes, thereby establishing a strong in-text contradiction within the document regarding the terms under which the Customer can engage with Kubient's introduced Third-Parties. This is in-text contradiction because the same paragraph contradicts itself. The definitions of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' and 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' both confirm this.

perturbation.original_text: All "Revenue" calculations shall refer to gross revenue actually received by the collecting party less client credits and allowances for returns, but without any deduction or offset for costs incurred by either Party, except for "Direct Deals".
perturbation.changed_text: All "Revenue" calculations shall refer to gross revenue actually received by the collecting party less client credits and allowances for returns, including deductions for marketing costs incurred to obtain that revenue, except for "Direct Deals".
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that revenue calculations should not include any deduction for costs incurred by either party, except for "Direct Deals". The modified text contradicts this by stating that deductions *should* be made for marketing costs incurred. This contradiction results in ambiguity and confusion regarding the calculation of revenue share.
perturbation.contradicted_text: *All "Revenue" calculations shall refer to gross revenue actually received by the collecting party less client credits and allowances for returns, including deductions for marketing costs incurred to obtain that revenue, except for "Direct Deals". For "Direct Deals", "Revenue" shall refer to Net Revenue, defined as gross revenue actually received by the collecting party less client credits and allowances for returns and less any agreed upon costs incurred by either Party in support of a "Direct Deal" for such things as production, photography, video, content creation and/or media buying (i.e. Nativo). Programmatic/Display refers to revenue generated by the following clients and any additional clients that AP adds during the term: Google Adexchange, Index Exchange, OpenX, Smaata, Rhythm One; Rubicon; TripleLift; Amazon; DistrictM; AppNexus; Xandr; Media.net; Criteo; Sovm; Saamba; AOL Marketplace
perturbation.justification: The original text states that revenue calculations should not include deductions for costs, while the changed text explicitly states that deductions *should* be made for marketing costs. This directly contradicts the original clause, creating ambiguity in revenue calculation, leading to a strong, obvious in-text contradiction.
